ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for Pu'er tea; the closest parent 'tea (beverage)' (FOODON_03301103) is used as the anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Pu'er tea code; 'Tea, infusion' (A00LC) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 16313001 'Tea (substance)' is the closest parent; no specific Pu'er tea concept exists in SNOMED CT.

main_ingredients: Pu'er tea leaves (Camellia sinensis var. assamica)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of brewed Pu'er tea, approximately 240 ml (240 g), served hot or warm without milk or sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, hot, leaf, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14355)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea (FDC ID 14355) is used as the closest approved analogue for brewed Pu'er tea. Pu'er is a post-fermented tea with a nutritional profile very similar to other brewed teas: essentially zero calories, protein, fat, and carbohydrates per 100 ml. Values are adapted from this entry. No dedicated Pu'er tea entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed tea contains negligible carbohydrates (approximately 0.3 g per 100 ml) and has no measurable glycemic response. Pu'er tea, like other plain brewed teas, is not listed individually in the University of Sydney GI Database because it contains no glycemic carbohydrates. By convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate content per serving is negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 ml); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed Pu'er tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension as it contains negligible sodium (approximately 7 mg per 240 ml serving) and may offer mild vasodilatory benefits from its polyphenol content; avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; no glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Pu'er tea without added sugar or milk has a negligible glycemic load and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; emerging evidence suggests Pu'er tea polyphenols may support insulin sensitivity, though it should be consumed unsweetened to avoid blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; polyphenol and theabrownin content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pu'er tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides polyphenols including theabrownins and catechins that have been associated with modest LDL-cholesterol reduction in observational studies; it is a heart-friendly beverage when consumed without added sugar or full-fat milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pu'er tea (普洱茶, Pǔ'ěr Chá) is a variety of post-fermented, aged Chinese tea originating from Yunnan Province, China, made from the large-leaf Camellia sinensis var. assamica cultivar. Unlike green or oolong teas, Pu'er undergoes a microbial fermentation process — either naturally over many years (raw/sheng Pu'er) or accelerated through pile-fermentation (ripe/shou Pu'er) — which gives it a distinctive earthy, smooth, and sometimes woody or musty flavour profile with a deep reddish-brown liquor. The tea is brewed by steeping compressed cakes, bricks, or loose leaves in hot water (typically 90–100°C) for 20–60 seconds per infusion, and can be re-steeped multiple times. Nutritionally, brewed Pu'er tea is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrates, and is composed almost entirely of water. It provides trace amounts of potassium, magnesium, and B vitamins. Pu'er tea is rich in polyphenols including theabrownins, gallic acid, and catechins formed during fermentation, which have been studied for potential benefits in lipid metabolism, gut microbiota modulation, and antioxidant activity. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when consumed plain and unsweetened. In Singapore, Pu'er tea is commonly served in Chinese seafood restaurants and dim sum establishments as a digestive aid and palate cleanser.

confidence_reason: No dedicated Pu'er tea entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed tea (FDC ID 14355), which is the closest approved analogue. The nutritional profile of brewed Pu'er tea is well-established as essentially zero-calorie and macro-free, consistent with all brewed plain teas, supporting medium confidence in the adapted values.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 14355 (brewed tea) as no dedicated Pu'er tea entry exists in primary regional databases. The brewed beverage profile is nutritionally equivalent to other plain brewed teas. 2. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed Pu'er tea contains no protein in the infusion. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed Pu'er tea contains no fat. sugar_g is confirmed zero for plain unsweetened brewed Pu'er tea per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as tea leaves are not consumed in the brewed beverage. 3. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per 240 ml serving is negligible (approximately 0.7 g). 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are set to 0.0 as brewed tea contains no fat fractions; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. Ontology codes use closest_parent matches as no dedicated Pu'er tea ontology terms exist in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 6. This record reflects the plain brewed beverage only; nutritional values will differ significantly if milk, sugar, or other additives are included. 7. The restaurant context (Riverfront Seafood - Boat Quay) and price (SGD 6.60) are noted from the source field survey record but are not used as nutritional evidence.
